channel.title: ret2libc on j0^35's blog
channel.description: Recent content in ret2libc on j0^35's blog

item.title: DUCTF-return-to-what
item.description: Leaking Libc addresses to do ret2libc with unknown libc First lets take a look at the binary mitigations {} ret2what checksec return-to-what [*] '/home/h4x5p4c3/Downloads/ret2what/return-to-what' Arch: amd64-64-little RELRO: Partial RELRO Stack: No canary found NX: NX enabled PIE: No PIE (0x400000) Nx is enabled so we can’t do a bof and place our shellcode to get a shell and no canary which makes it easier and ASLR is probably enabled. Disassembly and Decompilation pwndbg> disassemble main Dump of assembler code for function main: 0x00000000004011ad <+0>: push rbp 0x00000000004011ae <+1>: mov rbp,rsp # prologue 0x00000000004011b1 <+4>: lea rdi,[rip+0xe78] # rdi = address of [rip+0xe78] 0x00000000004011b8 <+11>: call 0x401030 <puts@plt> # call puts function 0x00000000004011bd <+16>: mov eax,0x0 # eax = 0x0 0x00000000004011c2 <+21>: call 0x401185 <vuln> # call vuln function which has the vulnerable gets function which we use to do bof 0x00000000004011c7 <+26>: mov eax,0x0 # eax = 0x0 0x00000000004011cc <+31>: pop rbp # epilogue 0x00000000004011cd <+32>: ret End of assembler dump.
